title: Minuteman - Lexington and Concord, 1775
opening: The tavern fire has burned low and nobody has gone home. Word came at midnight: Regulars are out of Boston, crossing the water, marching this way - after the powder stores at Concord, most likely, and after Mr. Hancock and Mr. Adams besides. Captain Parker stands by the door with his hat in his hands, counting heads. Your musket is loaded with the ball you cast last winter. Somewhere down the dark Boston road, you can just hear it now: drums.

squad.name: Capt. John Parker
squad.trait: calm, coughing, chooses words like coin

squad.name: Silas Hadley
squad.trait: strong as his anvil, slow to anger

squad.name: Old Amos
squad.trait: has seen battle and never talks about it

timelinePressure: 1775-04-19 The shot heard round the world at Lexington Green; the long retreat from Concord
1775-05-10 Fort Ticonderoga taken; cannon for the cause
1775-06-17 Bunker Hill - 'don't fire until you see the whites of their eyes'
1776-07-04 The Declaration is read aloud in every town square
